svg.title: 171Yb atom replacement, computation, and spin-resolved readout timing
svg.desc: Five semantic stages; reservoir maintenance and stationary-array computation run concurrently. The macrocycle follows Li et al. 2025, with separate preparation and readout microcycle scales.
svg.text: Li et al. (2025) macrocycle with preparation and spin-readout microcycles; horizontal widths are schematic unless a duration is printed.
batch n: load → qualify → prepare → handoff
receive batch n → compute during reload → spin-resolved readout
cool fresh atoms to about 10 µK
bright/dark = |0>; dark/bright = |1>; dark/dark = loss → replacement queue
1.8 µs trap modulation; four 900 ns subwindows shown; ×50 each stage; 0.7 ms total preparation.
Stage A: prepare ¹S₀, mF=+1/2 → Stage B: pump through 5d6s ³D₁ into ³P₀
Both optical stages occupy trap-off windows; repeated cycling recovers population that decays through ³P₁.
Raman transfer, de-pumping, and ordered images
One 1.8 µs modulation cycle is expanded below; the transfer/de-pump block repeats ×8 for each selected spin state.
649 and 770 nm are simultaneous; the following cycle de-pumps transferred ³P₂ population with 497 nm. Repeat ×8.
select |0> → map + depump 15 µs → image 4 ms → select |1> → map + depump 15 µs → image 4 ms → |0> / |1> / loss
Li et al. (2025): Figs. 2–4 and Appendix Fig. 9. Scheduled windows are apparatus-specific; pulse widths are not drawn in duration proportion.
Separate reference scale (Ma et al. 2023): RF π pulse 2.0 ms; resonant 302 nm Rydberg π pulse 330 ns. These do not set the Li macrocycle width.
